capabilities.name: vendor.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new vendor record in QuickBooks Online. Vendors are companies or individuals you pay (suppliers, contractors, service providers). The vendor_id returned can be used in bill.create to record money owed. Vendor names must be unique within QuickBooks - use vendor.search first to check for duplicates.

vendor_name.description: Display name for the vendor (must be unique in QuickBooks)

billing_address.description: Billing address with Line1, City, State, PostalCode

display_name.description: Confirmed display name as stored in QuickBooks

created_at.description: ISO timestamp when vendor was created

examples.description: Create a basic vendor with name and email

capabilities.name: vendor.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific vendor by ID. Use this to get current contact information, status, and other vendor details. The vendor_id should include the 'qb:' prefix (e.g., 'qb:123').

capabilities.name: vendor.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns a paginated list of vendors from QuickBooks. By default returns only active vendors. Use this for bulk retrieval or when you need to see all vendors. For finding a specific vendor by name, use vendor.search instead.

max_results.description: Maximum number of vendors to return (1-1000)

active_only.description: If true, only return active vendors

count.description: Number of vendors returned in this response

capabilities.name: vendor.search
capabilities.description: Search vendors by name with fuzzy matching
capabilities.description_detailed: Searches for vendors by name using fuzzy (LIKE) matching. Use this before vendor.create to check if a vendor already exists and avoid duplicates. Also useful for finding vendors when you only have a partial name.

search_term.description: Search term to match against vendor display names

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

count.description: Number of vendors matching the search

search_term.description: The search term that was used

examples.description: Search for vendors containing 'Office' in their name

capabilities.name: vendor.update
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates an existing vendor's information in QuickBooks. Only the fields provided will be updated - omitted fields retain their current values. QuickBooks requires the current SyncToken which is fetched automatically.

vendor_name.description: New display name (must be unique if changed)

capabilities.name: bill.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new bill (accounts payable) in QuickBooks. A bill represents money owed to a vendor for goods or services received. You must have a valid vendor_id (use vendor.search or vendor.create first). Line items specify what was purchased and can be linked to expense accounts or items.

line_items.description: Array of line items with Amount and detail (AccountRef or ItemRef)

due_date.description: Payment due date in YYYY-MM-DD format

txn_date.description: Transaction date in YYYY-MM-DD format (defaults to today)

examples.description: Create a bill for office supplies

capabilities.name: bill.get
capabilities.description: Get bill details including line items
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific bill including its line items, current balance, and payment status. Use this to check if a bill has been partially or fully paid.

balance.description: Remaining unpaid balance (0 if fully paid)

vendor_id.description: Vendor ID this bill is from

capabilities.name: bill.list
capabilities.description: List bills with optional vendor/date filters
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns a list of bills from QuickBooks with optional filtering. Use this to find bills for a specific vendor or to get a list of unpaid bills. Results are paginated.

vendor_id.description: Filter bills by vendor ID (with 'qb:' prefix)

unpaid_only.description: If true, only return bills with balance > 0

limit.description: Maximum number of bills to return

examples.description: List all unpaid bills for a vendor

capabilities.name: bill.payment.create
capabilities.description: Create a bill payment in QuickBooks
capabilities.description_detailed: Records a payment made to a vendor for one or more bills. This creates a BillPayment transaction in QuickBooks and reduces the balance on the linked bills. You can pay multiple bills at once by providing multiple bill_ids.

vendor_id.description: QuickBooks vendor ID receiving the payment (with 'qb:' prefix)

bill_ids.description: Array of bill IDs to apply payment to (with 'qb:' prefix)

check_num.description: Check number if payment_type is Check

txn_date.description: Payment date in YYYY-MM-DD format (defaults to today)

payment_id.description: QuickBooks bill payment ID with 'qb:' prefix

examples.description: Pay a bill with a check

capabilities.name: billpayment.list
capabilities.description: List vendor bill payments with optional filters
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns a list of bill payments (money paid to vendors) from QuickBooks. Can be filtered by vendor to see payment history for a specific supplier.

vendor_id.description: Filter payments by vendor ID (with 'qb:' prefix)

limit.description: Maximum number of payments to return

examples.description: List recent payments to a vendor

capabilities.name: qb.customer.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new customer record in QuickBooks Online. Customers are individuals or businesses who purchase goods or services from you. The customer_id returned can be used in invoice.create and payment.create. Customer names should be unique.

customer_name.description: Display name for the customer (should be unique)

company_name.description: Company/business name (if different from display name)

billing_address.description: Billing address with Line1, City, CountrySubDivisionCode, PostalCode

created_at.description: ISO timestamp when customer was created

capabilities.name: qb.customer.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific customer including contact info, current balance, and status. Use this to verify customer details before creating invoices or to check outstanding balances.

capabilities.name: qb.customer.update
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates an existing customer's information. Only provided fields are updated; omitted fields retain current values. Automatically handles SyncToken.

capabilities.name: qb.customer.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns a paginated list of customers. Use for bulk retrieval or browsing. For finding a specific customer by name, use qb.customer.search instead.

capabilities.name: qb.customer.search
capabilities.description: Search customers by name with fuzzy matching
capabilities.description_detailed: Searches for customers using LIKE matching on display name. Use before qb.customer.create to check for duplicates or to find customers by partial name.

search_term.description: Search term to match against customer names

capabilities.name: customer.create
capabilities.description: Create a customer in QuickBooks (MARS alias)
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new customer record in QuickBooks Online. Customers are individuals or businesses who purchase goods or services from you. The customer_id returned can be used in invoice.create and payment.create. Customer names should be unique.

customer_name.description: Display name for the customer (should be unique)

company_name.description: Company/business name (if different from display name)

billing_address.description: Billing address with Line1, City, CountrySubDivisionCode, PostalCode

created_at.description: ISO timestamp when customer was created

capabilities.name: customer.get
capabilities.description: Get customer details from QuickBooks (MARS alias)
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific customer including contact info, current balance, and status. Use this to verify customer details before creating invoices or to check outstanding balances.

capabilities.name: customer.update
capabilities.description: Update a customer in QuickBooks (MARS alias)
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates an existing customer's information. Only provided fields are updated; omitted fields retain current values. Automatically handles SyncToken.

capabilities.name: customer.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns a paginated list of customers. Use for bulk retrieval or browsing. For finding a specific customer by name, use qb.customer.search instead.

capabilities.name: qb.invoice.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new invoice (accounts receivable) for a customer. An invoice represents money owed to you for goods or services. Requires a valid customer_id and line items. The invoice can be sent via email using invoice.send after creation.

line_items.description: Array of line items with Amount, Description, and detail type

customer_memo.description: Message displayed on invoice to customer

examples.description: Create an invoice for consulting services

capabilities.name: qb.invoice.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves complete invoice details including line items, balance, and email status. Use to check payment status or get invoice details before sending.

capabilities.name: qb.invoice.send
capabilities.description: Send invoice via email through QuickBooks
capabilities.description_detailed: Sends an invoice to the customer via email using QuickBooks' email service. The email will be sent from your QuickBooks account and include a link for online payment.

email.description: Override email address (uses customer's email if not provided)

capabilities.name: qb.invoice.void
capabilities.description_detailed: Voids an existing invoice, setting its balance to zero. Use this instead of deleting to maintain audit trail. Cannot void invoices with payments applied.

void_reason.description: Reason for voiding (stored in private note)

capabilities.name: qb.invoice.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns a list of invoices with optional filtering by customer or payment status. For outstanding invoices specifically, use qb.invoice.list_outstanding.

capabilities.name: qb.invoice.list_outstanding
capabilities.description: List outstanding invoices (Balance > 0) - CRITICAL for payment allocation
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns only invoices with outstanding balances. Essential for MARS payment allocation workflows - identifies which invoices need payment.

capabilities.name: invoice.create
capabilities.description: Create an invoice in QuickBooks (MARS alias)
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new invoice (accounts receivable) for a customer. An invoice represents money owed to you for goods or services. Requires a valid customer_id and line items. The invoice can be sent via email using invoice.send after creation.

line_items.description: Array of line items with Amount, Description, and detail type

customer_memo.description: Message displayed on invoice to customer

capabilities.name: invoice.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves complete invoice details including line items, balance, and email status. Use to check payment status or get invoice details before sending.

capabilities.name: invoice.send
capabilities.description_detailed: Sends an invoice to the customer via email using QuickBooks' email service. The email will be sent from your QuickBooks account and include a link for online payment.

email.description: Override email address (uses customer's email if not provided)

capabilities.name: invoice.void
capabilities.description_detailed: Voids an existing invoice, setting its balance to zero. Use this instead of deleting to maintain audit trail. Cannot void invoices with payments applied.

void_reason.description: Reason for voiding (stored in private note)

capabilities.name: invoice.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns a list of invoices with optional filtering by customer or payment status. For outstanding invoices specifically, use qb.invoice.list_outstanding.

capabilities.name: item.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new item (product or service) that can be used on invoices and sales. Items can be Services, Inventory, or NonInventory types.

capabilities.name: qb.payment.create
capabilities.description: Create a payment (customer payment) in QuickBooks
capabilities.description_detailed: Records a payment received from a customer. Can be applied to specific invoices using invoice_ids, or recorded as unapplied payment.

invoice_ids.description: Invoice IDs to apply payment to

capabilities.name: payment.apply_to_invoice
capabilities.description: Apply payment to specific invoice(s) with multi-invoice allocation
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a payment and applies it to one or more invoices with specific amounts. Enables complex payment allocation scenarios where a single payment covers multiple invoices.

invoice_allocations.description: Array of {invoice_id, amount} for each invoice

capabilities.name: payment.list
capabilities.description: List customer payments with optional filters

capabilities.name: estimate.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a quote/estimate for a customer. Estimates can be converted to invoices and are useful for quoting before work begins.

capabilities.name: salesreceipt.create
capabilities.description: Create a sales receipt in QuickBooks
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a sales receipt for immediate payment transactions. Unlike invoices, sales receipts are used when payment is received at time of sale.

capabilities.name: creditmemo.create
capabilities.description: Create a credit memo in QuickBooks
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a credit memo to reduce a customer's balance. Can be applied to outstanding invoices or remain as available credit.

capabilities.name: timeactivity.create
capabilities.description: Create a time activity in QuickBooks
capabilities.description_detailed: Records time worked by an employee or vendor. Can be marked billable and linked to a customer for invoicing.

capabilities.name: refundreceipt.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a refund receipt to record money returned to a customer. Typically used after a sales receipt for returns.

capabilities.name: journal.create
capabilities.description: Create a journal entry in QuickBooks
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a manual journal entry with debit and credit lines. Journal entries must balance (total debits = total credits). Use for adjustments, corrections, or transactions not captured by other transaction types.

lines.description: Journal lines with PostingType, Amount, and AccountRef

examples.description: Create adjusting entry for accrued expenses

capabilities.name: qb.query
capabilities.description: Query QuickBooks entities using SQL-like syntax
capabilities.description_detailed: Execute SQL-like queries against QuickBooks entities. Supports SELECT with WHERE, ORDERBY, STARTPOSITION, and MAXRESULTS. Useful for complex queries not covered by other endpoints.

query.example: SELECT * FROM Invoice WHERE Balance > 0 MAXRESULTS 50

capabilities.name: chartofaccounts.get
capabilities.description: Get chart of accounts from QuickBooks
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves the full chart of accounts with optional filtering by account type. Use to get account IDs needed for journal entries, bills, and other transactions.

capabilities.name: account.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Alias for chartofaccounts.get - retrieves all accounts.

capabilities.name: account.get
capabilities.description: Get single account details with balance
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific account including current balance.

capabilities.name: purchaseorder.create
capabilities.description: Create a purchase order in QuickBooks
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a purchase order for a vendor. Purchase orders are used to order goods before receiving them and can be converted to bills upon receipt.

capabilities.name: expense.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a purchase/expense transaction. Unlike bills, expenses are paid immediately (cash, credit card, etc.) rather than on account.

capabilities.name: deposit.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a deposit to record money received into a bank account. Can include multiple line items from different sources.

deposit_account_id.description: Bank account ID to deposit into

line_items.description: Deposit line items with amounts and sources

capabilities.name: qb.transfer.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a transfer between two accounts (typically bank accounts). Records money moving from one account to another.

capabilities.name: transaction.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns a transaction list report showing all transactions within a date range. Useful for reconciliation and audit purposes.

capabilities.name: report.profitloss
capabilities.description_detailed: Generates a Profit & Loss (Income Statement) report showing revenue, expenses, and net income for a specified period. Can be filtered by class or location.

report.description: Full P&L report with rows, columns, and summary

capabilities.name: report.balancesheet
capabilities.description: Get Balance Sheet report from QuickBooks
capabilities.description_detailed: Generates a Balance Sheet showing assets, liabilities, and equity as of a specific date. Provides a snapshot of financial position.

as_of_date.description: Report as-of date (YYYY-MM-DD, defaults to today)

report.description: Balance sheet with assets, liabilities, equity sections

capabilities.name: report.profitloss.detail
capabilities.description: Get detailed P&L report with individual transactions
capabilities.description_detailed: Generates a detailed Profit & Loss report showing individual transactions that make up each account balance. More granular than standard P&L.

capabilities.name: report.cashflow
capabilities.description: Get Cash Flow Statement report from QuickBooks
capabilities.description_detailed: Generates a Cash Flow Statement showing cash from operating, investing, and financing activities. Shows how cash position changed over period.

report.description: Cash flow statement with operating/investing/financing sections

capabilities.name: report.generalledger
capabilities.description_detailed: Generates a General Ledger report showing all transactions by account. The most detailed transaction report available.

report.description: General ledger with all account transactions

capabilities.name: report.ar_aging
capabilities.description_detailed: Generates an Accounts Receivable Aging Summary showing customer balances grouped by aging buckets (Current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days).

report.description: AR aging summary by customer and bucket

capabilities.name: report.ap_aging
capabilities.description_detailed: Generates an Accounts Payable Aging Summary showing vendor balances grouped by aging buckets. Helps prioritize vendor payments.

report.description: AP aging summary by vendor and bucket

capabilities.name: report.ar_aging_detail
capabilities.description: Get detailed AR Aging with individual invoices
capabilities.description_detailed: Generates a detailed Accounts Receivable Aging report showing each individual invoice and its age. More granular than summary.

report.description: Detailed AR aging with invoice-level detail

capabilities.name: report.ap_aging_detail
capabilities.description: Get detailed AP Aging with individual bills
capabilities.description_detailed: Generates a detailed Accounts Payable Aging report showing each individual bill and its age. Useful for payment prioritization.

report.description: Detailed AP aging with bill-level detail

capabilities.name: budget.get
capabilities.description: Get budget data for variance analysis (actual vs budget comparison)
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves budget data from QuickBooks for comparison against actual performance. Use for variance analysis and financial planning.

fiscal_year.description: Fiscal year to retrieve budget for

capabilities.name: document.upload
capabilities.description: Upload document (W-9, invoice, receipt) and attach to entity
capabilities.description_detailed: Uploads a document file and attaches it to a QuickBooks entity like a vendor, customer, bill, or invoice. Useful for storing W-9s, receipts, and source documents.

file_path.description: Local path to the file to upload

entity_type.description: Type of entity to attach to

capabilities.name: class.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves all classes defined in QuickBooks. Classes are used for categorization and tracking (e.g., by project, product line, or business segment).

capabilities.name: department.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves all departments (locations) defined in QuickBooks. Departments are used for location tracking and reporting.

capabilities.name: employee.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves all employees defined in QuickBooks. Employees can be assigned to time activities and used for tracking billable work.

capabilities.name: employee.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed information about a specific employee.

capabilities.name: term.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves all payment terms defined in QuickBooks (e.g., Net 30, Due on Receipt). Terms define when payment is due and any early payment discounts.

capabilities.name: taxcode.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves all tax codes defined in QuickBooks. Tax codes determine how sales tax is calculated on transactions.

capabilities.name: company.info
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves company profile information including name, address, contact details, and fiscal year settings.

capabilities.name: netsuite.journal.create
capabilities.description: Create a journal entry in NetSuite
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a journal entry in NetSuite's general ledger. CRITICAL: Total debits must equal total credits or the entry will be rejected. Each line requires an account ID and either a debit or credit amount. Uses REST API with TBA or OAuth 2.0 authentication.

subsidiary_id.description: Internal ID of the subsidiary (OneWorld only)

lines.description: Journal entry lines (each with account_id and debit OR credit)

memo.description: Header memo for the journal entry

capabilities.name: netsuite.journal.get
capabilities.description: Get a journal entry by ID
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves a journal entry from NetSuite by its internal ID. Returns full details including all line items with account information, debits, credits, and any segment values (department, class, location).

journal_entry_id.description: NetSuite internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

lines.description: Line items with account, debit/credit amounts

capabilities.name: netsuite.vendorbill.create
capabilities.description: Create a vendor bill in NetSuite
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a vendor bill (accounts payable) in NetSuite. Vendor bills track money owed to suppliers. Each bill has expense lines with account allocations. Bills can be approved and paid via vendor payments.

vendor_id.description: Vendor internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

expense_lines.description: Expense line items with account_id and amount

capabilities.name: netsuite.vendorbill.get
capabilities.description: Get a vendor bill by ID
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves a vendor bill from NetSuite by its internal ID. Returns full details including expense lines, payment status, and approval status.

bill_id.description: Vendor bill internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

capabilities.name: netsuite.vendorbill.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Lists vendor bills using SuiteQL query. Supports filtering by vendor, date range, and status. Returns up to 1000 results per page.

capabilities.name: netsuite.vendorbill.approve
capabilities.description_detailed: Sets the approval status of a vendor bill to Approved. Only applicable if bill approvals are enabled in NetSuite. Approved bills can then be scheduled for payment.

bill_id.description: Vendor bill internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

capabilities.name: netsuite.vendor.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves a vendor record from NetSuite by its internal ID. Returns company information, contact details, and current balance.

vendor_id.description: Vendor internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

capabilities.name: netsuite.vendor.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new vendor record in NetSuite. Vendors are entities you pay - suppliers, contractors, service providers. The vendor_id returned can be used to create bills and payments.

account_number.description: Your account number with this vendor

capabilities.name: netsuite.vendor.update
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates an existing vendor record in NetSuite. Can update contact info, address, payment terms, and other fields. Only provided fields are updated - others remain unchanged.

vendor_id.description: Vendor internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

address.description: Address object with street1, street2, city, state, zip, country

account_number.description: Your account number with this vendor

capabilities.name: netsuite.vendor.search
capabilities.description: Search for vendors by company name
capabilities.description_detailed: Searches for vendors in NetSuite by company name using SuiteQL. Returns matching vendors with basic info. Use before creating vendors to avoid duplicates.

vendor_name.description: Company name to search for (partial match supported)

capabilities.name: netsuite.vendor.upload_document
capabilities.description_detailed: Uploads a document (W-9, contract, etc.) to NetSuite File Cabinet and attaches it to a vendor record. File content must be base64 encoded.

vendor_id.description: Vendor the file is attached to

capabilities.name: netsuite.payment.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a vendor payment in NetSuite to pay one or more bills. Specify the vendor, bank account, and bills to pay. Payments can be partial or full payment of bills.

vendor_id.description: Vendor internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

bank_account_id.description: Bank account internal ID to pay from

bills_to_pay.description: Bills to pay with optional amounts

payment_method.description: Payment method internal ID (check, ACH, wire, etc.)

capabilities.name: netsuite.payment.batch
capabilities.description_detailed: Processes multiple vendor payments in a batch. Each payment is processed independently - failures don't affect others. Returns results and errors for each payment.

payments.description: Array of payment specifications (same format as payment.create)

capabilities.name: netsuite.purchaseorder.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a purchase order in NetSuite. POs are used to order items from vendors. When received, POs can be converted to vendor bills.

vendor_id.description: Vendor internal ID (with or without 'ns:' prefix)

item_lines.description: Item lines with item_id, quantity, and optional rate

capabilities.name: netsuite.account.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves the chart of accounts from NetSuite using SuiteQL. Returns account numbers, names, types, and balances. Use to look up account IDs for journal entries and bills.

account_type.description: Filter by account type (Bank, Expense, Income, etc.)

accounts.description: List of accounts with id, number, name, type, balance

capabilities.name: netsuite.subsidiary.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves the list of subsidiaries from a NetSuite OneWorld account. Subsidiary IDs are required for journal entries and transactions. Non-OneWorld accounts have a single implicit subsidiary.

subsidiaries.description: List of subsidiaries with id, name, country

capabilities.name: netsuite.gl.transactions
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves general ledger transaction lines from NetSuite using SuiteQL. Essential for bank reconciliation workflows - matches bank statement lines to GL entries. Filter by account and date range.

account_ids.description: Account internal IDs to filter (omit for all accounts)

transactions.description: GL transaction lines with account, debit/credit, memo

capabilities.name: netsuite.reconcile.bank
capabilities.description_detailed: Compares bank statement balance against NetSuite GL balance for a specific account and date. Returns variance information. Note: Actual reconciliation is typically done in NetSuite UI.

capabilities.name: netsuite.query
capabilities.description_detailed: Executes a SuiteQL query against NetSuite. SuiteQL is NetSuite's SQL-like query language. Max 100,000 total results, 1,000 per page. Use for custom data retrieval not covered by other capabilities.

query.example: SELECT id, companyname FROM vendor WHERE isinactive = 'F'

capabilities.name: netsuite.customrecord.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a custom record in NetSuite. Custom records are user-defined record types created in NetSuite. Record type ID format: customrecord_XXX

fields.description: Field values for the custom record

capabilities.name: stripe.payment.create
capabilities.description: Create a payment intent in Stripe
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a PaymentIntent object to represent your intent to collect payment. PaymentIntents track the lifecycle of a payment from creation through checkout. Use for one-time payments; for recurring, use subscriptions instead.

amount.description: Amount in smallest currency unit (cents for USD)

customer.description: Stripe customer ID to associate with payment

description.description: Description for the payment (appears in dashboard)

metadata.description: Key-value pairs for your own use

capabilities.name: stripe.payment.retrieve
capabilities.description: Retrieve a payment intent in Stripe
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves the details of a PaymentIntent that was previously created. Use to check payment status or get details for reconciliation.

capabilities.name: stripe.payment.refund
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a refund for a PaymentIntent or Charge. You can refund the full amount or a partial amount. Refunds are processed immediately but may take 5-10 business days to appear on customer's statement.

charge.description: Charge ID to refund (alternative to payment_intent)

amount.description: Amount to refund in cents (defaults to full amount)

metadata.description: Key-value pairs for your own use

capabilities.name: stripe.refund.update
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates the metadata on a refund. Other fields cannot be changed.

capabilities.name: stripe.refund.cancel
capabilities.description_detailed: Cancels a refund with a status of requires_action. You cannot cancel refunds that have already been processed.

capabilities.name: stripe.charge.retrieve
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves the details of a charge. Charges are created when a PaymentIntent is confirmed and payment is attempted.

created.description: Filter by created date (gte, lte, gt, lt)

capabilities.name: stripe.customer.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new customer object in Stripe. Customers allow you to perform recurring charges, save payment methods, and track payments. Creating a customer is often the first step before taking payments.

metadata.description: Key-value pairs for your own use

payment_method.description: Payment method to attach to customer

examples.description: Create a customer with email and metadata

capabilities.name: stripe.customer.retrieve
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves the details of an existing customer by ID.

capabilities.name: stripe.customer.update
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates the specified customer by setting parameter values.

capabilities.name: stripe.customer.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns a list of customers in reverse chronological order.

created.description: Filter by created date (gte, lte, gt, lt)

capabilities.name: stripe.customer.delete
capabilities.description_detailed: Permanently deletes a customer and cancels any active subscriptions. This cannot be undone. Also immediately cancels any active subscriptions.

capabilities.name: stripe.customer.search
capabilities.description_detailed: Search for customers using Stripe's Search API. Supports searching by email, name, phone, metadata, and more using query syntax.

capabilities.name: stripe.product.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new product object in Stripe. Products represent goods or services you sell. After creating a product, create prices to define how much to charge.

active.description: Whether product is available for purchase

metadata.description: Key-value pairs for your own use

tax_code.description: Tax code for automatic tax calculation

examples.description: Create a SaaS product with default price

capabilities.name: stripe.product.delete
capabilities.description_detailed: Deletes a product. Products can only be deleted if they have no prices associated with them.

capabilities.name: stripe.price.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new price for a product. Prices define how much and how often to charge. A product can have multiple prices (e.g., monthly vs annual).

product.description: Product ID this price is for

metadata.description: Key-value pairs for your own use

capabilities.name: stripe.price.update
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates a price. Most fields are immutable after creation - you can only update metadata, nickname, and active status.

capabilities.name: stripe.subscription.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new subscription for a customer. Subscriptions automatically charge customers on a recurring basis. Requires a customer with a payment method.

items.description: List of subscription items with price IDs

default_payment_method.description: Payment method to use for this subscription

trial_period_days.description: Number of trial days before charging

cancel_at_period_end.description: Cancel at end of current period

billing_cycle_anchor.description: Unix timestamp for billing cycle start

metadata.description: Key-value pairs for your own use

capabilities.name: stripe.subscription.update
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates an existing subscription. Can change items (upgrade/downgrade), payment method, billing settings, and more.

cancel_at_period_end.description: Set to cancel at period end

trial_end.description: Unix timestamp or 'now' to end trial

capabilities.name: stripe.subscription.cancel
capabilities.description_detailed: Cancels a subscription. By default, cancels immediately. Set cancel_at_period_end to cancel at the end of the current billing period.

cancel_at_period_end.description: Cancel at end of period instead of immediately

capabilities.name: stripe.invoice.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a draft invoice for a customer. Invoices are automatically created for subscriptions, but you can also create one-off invoices manually.

days_until_due.description: Days until payment is due (if send_invoice)

metadata.description: Key-value pairs for your own use

capabilities.name: stripe.invoice.update
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates a draft invoice. Cannot update finalized invoices.

capabilities.name: stripe.invoice.finalize
capabilities.description_detailed: Finalizes a draft invoice, transitioning it to 'open' status. Finalized invoices can no longer be edited and are ready for payment.

capabilities.name: stripe.invoice.pay
capabilities.description_detailed: Attempts to pay an open invoice using the customer's default payment method or a specified payment method.

payment_method.description: Payment method to use (uses default if not specified)

source.description: Card source to use (deprecated, use payment_method)

capabilities.name: stripe.invoice.send
capabilities.description_detailed: Sends an open invoice to the customer via email. The invoice must be finalized before it can be sent.

capabilities.name: stripe.invoice.void
capabilities.description_detailed: Voids an open invoice. Voided invoices cannot be paid and are marked as void.

capabilities.name: stripe.invoice.delete
capabilities.description: Delete a draft invoice in Stripe
capabilities.description_detailed: Permanently deletes a draft invoice. Only draft invoices can be deleted.

capabilities.name: stripe.checkout.session.create
capabilities.description: Create a Checkout Session in Stripe
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a Checkout Session for collecting payment. Stripe Checkout is a pre-built, hosted payment page that handles card input, validation, and 3D Secure authentication. Supports one-time and subscription payments.

success_url.description: URL to redirect after successful payment

cancel_url.description: URL to redirect if customer cancels

line_items.description: Line items for the checkout (price or price_data)

client_reference_id.description: Your reference ID for this session

metadata.description: Key-value pairs for your own use

url.description: Checkout page URL to redirect customer

capabilities.name: stripe.checkout.session.retrieve
capabilities.description: Retrieve a Checkout Session in Stripe
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves a Checkout Session. Use to verify payment completion after customer returns from the hosted checkout page.

capabilities.name: stripe.checkout.session.expire
capabilities.description: Expire a Checkout Session in Stripe
capabilities.description_detailed: Expires an open Checkout Session, preventing it from being completed. Useful for invalidating sessions that are no longer valid.

capabilities.name: stripe.checkout.lineitems.list
capabilities.description: List line items for a Checkout Session
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves the line items for a Checkout Session. Line items show what the customer purchased or will purchase.

data.description: List of line items with price, quantity, amount

capabilities.name: stripe.paymentmethod.create
capabilities.description: Create a payment method in Stripe
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a PaymentMethod object. PaymentMethods represent your customer's payment instruments. They can be attached to customers for future payments.

metadata.description: Key-value pairs for your own use

capabilities.name: stripe.paymentmethod.retrieve
capabilities.description: Retrieve a payment method in Stripe

capabilities.name: stripe.paymentmethod.update
capabilities.description: Update a payment method in Stripe

capabilities.name: stripe.paymentmethod.list
capabilities.description: List payment methods for a customer

capabilities.name: stripe.paymentmethod.attach
capabilities.description: Attach a payment method to a customer
capabilities.description_detailed: Attaches a PaymentMethod to a Customer. This allows the payment method to be reused for future payments.

capabilities.name: stripe.paymentmethod.detach
capabilities.description: Detach a payment method from a customer

capabilities.name: stripe.dispute.retrieve
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves a dispute (chargeback) initiated by a customer's bank. Disputes require evidence submission to challenge.

capabilities.name: stripe.dispute.update
capabilities.description: Update a dispute with evidence in Stripe
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates a dispute with evidence to challenge it. You can submit various types of evidence to support your case.

submit.description: Submit evidence for review (cannot be undone)

capabilities.name: stripe.dispute.close
capabilities.description_detailed: Closes a dispute, accepting the customer's claim. This action cannot be undone and the disputed amount will be returned to the customer.

capabilities.name: stripe.balance.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves the current account balance, showing available and pending amounts by currency. Use to check how much can be paid out.

capabilities.name: stripe.balance.transactions.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns a list of transactions that have contributed to the Stripe account balance. Includes charges, payouts, refunds, transfers, etc.

source.description: Filter by source ID (charge, refund, etc.)

created.description: Filter by created date (gte, lte, gt, lt)

capabilities.name: stripe.payout.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Returns a list of payouts to your bank account. Payouts are the movement of funds from Stripe to your external account.

capabilities.name: stripe.transfer.create
capabilities.description: Create a transfer to connected account (Connect)
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a transfer to a connected Stripe account. Requires Stripe Connect. Use for marketplace payouts to sellers or service providers.

metadata.description: Key-value pairs for your own use

capabilities.name: stripe.webhook.create
capabilities.description: Create a webhook endpoint in Stripe
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a webhook endpoint that Stripe will send events to. Webhooks notify your application of events like successful payments, subscription changes, and dispute updates.

enabled_events.description: Events to send to this endpoint

capabilities.name: stripe.event.retrieve
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves the details of an event. Events are created when something interesting happens in your account (payment succeeds, subscription renews, etc).

capabilities.name: stripe.terminal.reader.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Registers a new Terminal reader to your Stripe account. Readers are physical devices that accept in-person payments.

location.description: Location ID to assign reader to

capabilities.name: stripe.terminal.reader.process_payment
capabilities.description: Process a payment on a Terminal reader
capabilities.description_detailed: Initiates a payment on a Terminal reader. The reader will prompt the customer to present their card.

capabilities.name: stripe.terminal.reader.cancel_action
capabilities.description: Cancel the current action on a reader

capabilities.name: stripe.terminal.location.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a location representing a physical place where Terminal readers are deployed. Useful for organizing readers across multiple stores.

capabilities.name: stripe.terminal.connection_token.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a connection token for the Terminal SDK. Tokens are used to connect your application to Terminal readers.

location.description: Location to scope the token to

capabilities.name: stripe.issuing.card.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new Issuing card for a cardholder. Cards can be physical or virtual. Physical cards are shipped to the cardholder's address.

capabilities.name: stripe.issuing.cardholder.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new Issuing cardholder. Cardholders represent individuals or businesses that can be issued cards.

capabilities.name: stripe.issuing.authorization.retrieve
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves an authorization representing a request to authorize a purchase on an Issuing card.

capabilities.name: stripe.issuing.authorization.approve
capabilities.description_detailed: Approves a pending authorization. Only works for real-time authorizations when you have real-time authorization controls enabled.

capabilities.name: stripe.issuing.transaction.retrieve
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves a transaction representing a completed purchase on an Issuing card.

capabilities.name: stripe.issuing.dispute.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a dispute for an Issuing transaction. Use when a cardholder disputes a charge on their Issuing card.

capabilities.name: stripe.issuing.dispute.submit
capabilities.description: Submit an Issuing dispute for review

capabilities.name: stripe.treasury.financial_account.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a FinancialAccount for holding funds. Treasury financial accounts provide banking-like functionality including account numbers and routing numbers.

capabilities.name: stripe.treasury.outbound_payment.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates an outbound payment from a FinancialAccount to an external destination (bank account, debit card, or Stripe account).

capabilities.name: stripe.treasury.outbound_transfer.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates an outbound transfer from a FinancialAccount to a connected Stripe account's external bank account.

capabilities.name: stripe.identity.verification_session.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a VerificationSession for collecting and verifying identity documents. Sessions guide users through document capture and selfie verification using Stripe's hosted UI or embedded SDK.

capabilities.name: stripe.identity.verification_session.redact
capabilities.description_detailed: Redacts a VerificationSession to remove all collected identity data. Use for GDPR compliance or when data is no longer needed.

capabilities.name: stripe.identity.verification_report.retrieve
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves a VerificationReport containing the results of identity verification including document data and selfie match results.

capabilities.name: stripe.radar.early_fraud_warning.retrieve
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves an early fraud warning indicating a charge is potentially fraudulent. Issued when card networks report suspected fraud.

capabilities.name: stripe.radar.review.retrieve
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves a review generated by Radar when a payment is flagged for manual review based on your rules.

capabilities.name: stripe.radar.review.approve
capabilities.description_detailed: Approves a payment that was placed in review. The payment will proceed.

capabilities.name: stripe.radar.value_list.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a value list for use in Radar rules. Value lists can contain email addresses, IP addresses, card fingerprints, or strings.

item_type.description: Type of items in the list

capabilities.name: stripe.radar.value_list_item.create
capabilities.description: Add an item to a Radar value list

capabilities.name: stripe.radar.value_list_item.list
capabilities.description: List items in a value list

capabilities.name: stripe.tax.calculation.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Calculates tax for a transaction. Returns the tax amounts that should be collected based on the customer's location and the items being sold.

capabilities.name: stripe.tax.calculation.line_items.list
capabilities.description: List line items for a tax calculation

capabilities.name: stripe.tax.transaction.create_from_calculation
capabilities.description: Create a tax transaction from a calculation
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a tax transaction from an existing calculation. Use this to record the tax collected when completing a payment.

capabilities.name: stripe.tax.transaction.create_reversal
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a reversal for a tax transaction. Use when refunding a payment to properly account for the tax reversal.

capabilities.name: stripe.tax.transaction.line_items.list
capabilities.description: List line items for a tax transaction

capabilities.name: stripe.tax.settings.retrieve
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves your Stripe Tax settings including head office location and default tax behavior.

capabilities.name: stripe.tax.registration.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a tax registration indicating you're registered to collect tax in a jurisdiction. Required for accurate tax calculations.

capabilities.name: stripe.coupon.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a coupon that can be applied to customers, subscriptions, or invoices to provide a discount.

capabilities.name: stripe.coupon.delete
capabilities.description_detailed: Deletes a coupon. Existing discounts using the coupon remain valid.

capabilities.name: stripe.promotion_code.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a promotion code that customers can enter to apply a coupon. Promotion codes can have restrictions like customer, date, or usage limits.

capabilities.name: stripe.setup_intent.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a SetupIntent for collecting payment method details without charging. Use to save cards for future payments like subscriptions or metered billing.

customer.description: Customer to attach payment method to

usage.description: How payment method will be used

payment_method.description: Payment method to attach and confirm

capabilities.name: stripe.setup_intent.confirm
capabilities.description_detailed: Confirms a SetupIntent to complete the payment method setup process. May require customer authentication for 3D Secure.

capabilities.name: stripe.payment_link.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a shareable payment link for no-code payment collection. Customers can pay by visiting the URL without needing a website.

capabilities.name: stripe.payment_link.line_items.list
capabilities.description: List line items for a Payment Link

capabilities.name: stripe.quote.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a quote for a customer. Quotes allow you to send pricing proposals that customers can accept to start a subscription or payment.

capabilities.name: stripe.quote.finalize
capabilities.description_detailed: Finalizes a quote, preventing further edits and generating a PDF. Finalized quotes can be sent to customers for acceptance.

capabilities.name: stripe.quote.accept
capabilities.description_detailed: Accepts a quote on behalf of the customer, creating a subscription or invoice based on the quote configuration.

capabilities.name: stripe.credit_note.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a credit note for an invoice. Credit notes reduce the amount owed and can refund customers or create account credit.

credit_amount.description: Amount to add as customer credit

capabilities.name: stripe.billing_portal.session.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a portal session for a customer to manage their subscription, update payment methods, and view invoices in a self-service interface.

return_url.description: URL to return to after portal

capabilities.name: stripe.billing_portal.configuration.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a configuration defining what customers can do in the portal, such as updating subscriptions, payment methods, or viewing invoices.

capabilities.name: stripe.customer.tax_id.create
capabilities.description: Add a tax ID to a customer
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a tax ID for a customer. Tax IDs are used for tax compliance and appear on invoices.

tax_id.description: Tax ID object ID to delete

capabilities.name: stripe.financial_connections.session.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a session for a customer to connect their bank account. The session provides a secure interface for account linking.

return_url.description: URL to return to after linking

capabilities.name: stripe.financial_connections.account.refresh
capabilities.description_detailed: Refreshes the data for a linked account including balance and transaction information.

capabilities.name: stripe.file.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Uploads a file to Stripe for use in disputes, identity verification, or account onboarding.

capabilities.name: stripe.file_link.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a shareable link for a file that can be accessed without authentication for a limited time.

capabilities.name: stripe.reporting.report_run.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new report run to generate a report of a specific type. Reports are generated asynchronously.

capabilities.name: stripe.account.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new connected account for Stripe Connect. Connected accounts represent your platform's sellers, service providers, or vendors.

capabilities.name: stripe.account.delete
capabilities.description_detailed: Deletes a connected account. Only Custom or Express accounts created by your platform can be deleted.

capabilities.name: stripe.account_link.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates an account link for onboarding a connected account. Links redirect users to Stripe-hosted onboarding pages.

refresh_url.description: URL if link expires or fails

capabilities.name: stripe.account.login_link.create
capabilities.description: Create a login link for Express Dashboard
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a single-use login link for an Express connected account to access their Stripe Express Dashboard.

capabilities.name: stripe.application_fee.retrieve
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves an application fee collected on a payment from a connected account.

capabilities.name: plaid.link.create
capabilities.description: Create Link token for Plaid initialization
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a Link token to initialize Plaid Link in the client application. Link is the client-side component that users interact with to connect their bank accounts. The token is short-lived and must be used immediately.

products.description: Plaid products to enable (transactions, auth, identity, balance)

client_name.description: Name displayed to user during Link flow

capabilities.name: plaid.accesstoken.exchange
capabilities.description: Exchange public token for access token
capabilities.description_detailed: Exchanges the public_token returned by Plaid Link for a permanent access_token. This access_token is used for all subsequent API calls to retrieve account data. Must be stored securely - it provides full access to the linked account.

public_token.description: Public token from Plaid Link onSuccess callback

access_token.description: Permanent access token for API calls (store securely)

item_id.description: Unique identifier for the Item (linked institution)

capabilities.name: plaid.transactions.sync
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves transactions using the /transactions/sync endpoint (recommended method). Returns added, modified, and removed transactions since the last sync. Use cursor-based pagination for efficient incremental updates.

access_token.description: Plaid access token for the linked account

cursor.description: Cursor from previous sync for incremental updates

count.description: Number of transactions to fetch per page

transactions_removed.description: Transaction IDs that have been removed

next_cursor.description: Cursor for next page (save for incremental sync)

capabilities.name: plaid.transactions.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves transactions using the /transactions/sync endpoint (recommended method). Returns added, modified, and removed transactions since the last sync. Use cursor-based pagination for efficient incremental updates.

access_token.description: Plaid access token for the linked account

cursor.description: Cursor from previous sync for incremental updates

count.description: Number of transactions to fetch per page

transactions_removed.description: Transaction IDs that have been removed

next_cursor.description: Cursor for next page (save for incremental sync)

capabilities.name: plaid.accounts.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves the list of accounts associated with an Item (linked institution). Returns account names, types, subtypes, and masked account numbers. Use this to display available accounts before fetching balances or transactions.

access_token.description: Plaid access token for the linked account

accounts.description: List of accounts with id, name, type, subtype, mask

capabilities.name: plaid.balance.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves real-time balance information for accounts. Unlike cached data, this makes a live request to the institution. Returns current balance, available balance, and credit limit where applicable.

access_token.description: Plaid access token for the linked account

account_ids.description: Specific account IDs to fetch (all if omitted)

accounts.description: Accounts with current_balance, available_balance, limit, currency

capabilities.name: plaid.auth.get
capabilities.description: Get bank account numbers (with TANs support)
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves account and routing numbers for ACH and wire transfers. Some institutions use tokenized account numbers (TANs) for security. Requires the 'auth' product to be enabled during Link initialization.

access_token.description: Plaid access token with auth product enabled

accounts.description: Accounts with routing, account, wire_routing numbers

numbers.description: Raw ACH, EFT, and international numbers

capabilities.name: plaid.identity.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves identity information for account holders including names, addresses, phone numbers, and email addresses. Useful for KYC/AML verification. Requires the 'identity' product to be enabled.

access_token.description: Plaid access token with identity product enabled

accounts.description: Accounts with owners array containing identity data

capabilities.name: plaid.transfer.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates an ACH transfer to move money between accounts using Plaid Transfer. Supports debit (pull money from user) and credit (push money to user) transfers. Same-day ACH available with 'same-day-ach' network option.

access_token.description: Plaid access token for the source/destination account

user_legal_name.description: Legal name of the account holder

idempotency_key.description: Unique key to prevent duplicate transfers

expected_funds_available_date.description: Expected date funds will be available

capabilities.name: plaid.transfer.get
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves the current status and details of a transfer. Use this to track transfer progress and handle failures.

status.description: Current status (pending, posted, cancelled, failed, returned)

cancellable.description: Whether transfer can still be cancelled

capabilities.name: plaid.processor.token.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a processor token to share account access with a third-party processor (Stripe, Dwolla, etc.) without exposing the access_token. The processor token is single-use and scoped to the specified account and processor.

access_token.description: Plaid access token for the account

account_id.description: Account ID to create token for

processor_token.description: Token to provide to the processor

capabilities.name: crm.contact.create
capabilities.description: Create a contact in HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new contact record in HubSpot CRM. Contacts represent individual people you interact with.

capabilities.name: crm.contact.get
capabilities.description: Get contact details from HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves details for a specific contact by ID.

contact_id.description: HubSpot contact ID (with or without hs: prefix)

capabilities.name: crm.contact.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves a list of contacts from HubSpot CRM.

limit.description: Maximum number of contacts to return

capabilities.name: crm.contact.update
capabilities.description: Update a contact in HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates an existing contact's properties in HubSpot.

contact_id.description: HubSpot contact ID (with or without hs: prefix)

capabilities.name: crm.contact.delete
capabilities.description: Delete a contact from HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Permanently deletes a contact from HubSpot.

contact_id.description: HubSpot contact ID (with or without hs: prefix)

capabilities.name: crm.contact.search
capabilities.description_detailed: Searches for contacts using HubSpot's search API. Supports full-text search across contact properties.

capabilities.name: crm.deal.create
capabilities.description: Create a deal in HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new deal record in HubSpot CRM. Deals represent potential revenue opportunities in your sales pipeline.

capabilities.name: crm.deal.get
capabilities.description: Get deal details from HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves details for a specific deal by ID.

deal_id.description: HubSpot deal ID (with or without hs: prefix)

capabilities.name: crm.deal.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves a list of deals from HubSpot CRM.

limit.description: Maximum number of deals to return

capabilities.name: crm.deal.update
capabilities.description: Update a deal in HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates an existing deal's properties in HubSpot.

capabilities.name: crm.deal.delete
capabilities.description: Delete a deal from HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Permanently deletes a deal from HubSpot.

capabilities.name: crm.deal.search
capabilities.description_detailed: Searches for deals using HubSpot's search API.

capabilities.name: crm.company.create
capabilities.description: Create a company in HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new company record in HubSpot CRM. Companies represent organizations you do business with.

capabilities.name: crm.company.get
capabilities.description: Get company details from HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves details for a specific company by ID.

company_id.description: HubSpot company ID (with or without hs: prefix)

capabilities.name: crm.company.list
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves a list of companies from HubSpot CRM.

limit.description: Maximum number of companies to return

capabilities.name: crm.company.update
capabilities.description: Update a company in HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Updates an existing company's properties in HubSpot.

capabilities.name: crm.company.delete
capabilities.description: Delete a company from HubSpot CRM
capabilities.description_detailed: Permanently deletes a company from HubSpot.

capabilities.name: crm.company.search
capabilities.description_detailed: Searches for companies using HubSpot's search API.

capabilities.name: message.send
capabilities.description: Send a message to a Slack channel
capabilities.description_detailed: Posts a message to a Slack channel. Supports rich formatting with Block Kit and threaded replies.

channel.description: Channel ID or name (e.g., C1234567890 or #general)

blocks.description: Block Kit blocks for rich formatting

capabilities.name: message.direct
capabilities.description: Send a direct message on Slack
capabilities.description_detailed: Opens a DM conversation with a user and sends a message. Creates the DM channel if it doesn't exist.

capabilities.name: file.upload
capabilities.description_detailed: Uploads a file to one or more Slack channels. Can include an initial comment with the file.

channels.description: Comma-separated channel IDs to share to

content.description: File content (base64 encoded or raw text)

title.description: Title for the file (defaults to filename)

initial_comment.description: Message to post with the file

capabilities.name: channel.create
capabilities.description_detailed: Creates a new public or private Slack channel. Channel names must be lowercase, without spaces.

capabilities.name: channel.invite
capabilities.description: Invite users to a Slack channel
capabilities.description_detailed: Invites one or more users to a Slack channel. Users must be part of the workspace.

capabilities.name: channel.history
capabilities.description: Get message history from a Slack channel
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves recent messages from a Slack channel. Returns messages in reverse chronological order.

channel_id.description: Channel ID to get history from

limit.description: Maximum number of messages to return

capabilities.name: channel.list
capabilities.description: List all channels in the workspace
capabilities.description_detailed: Lists all channels in the Slack workspace that the bot has access to. Can filter by public/private channels.

limit.description: Maximum number of channels to return

channels.description: List of channel objects with id, name, is_private, is_archived

capabilities.name: channel.archive
capabilities.description_detailed: Archives a Slack channel. Archived channels are hidden from the channel list but can be unarchived later. Requires appropriate permissions.

capabilities.name: channel.topic
capabilities.description: Set the topic for a Slack channel
capabilities.description_detailed: Sets or updates the topic for a Slack channel. The topic is displayed at the top of the channel.

capabilities.name: reaction.add
capabilities.description: Add a reaction to a message
capabilities.description_detailed: Adds an emoji reaction to a message. The emoji name should be specified without colons (e.g., 'thumbsup' not ':thumbsup:').

channel_id.description: Channel ID where the message is

capabilities.name: reaction.remove
capabilities.description: Remove a reaction from a message
capabilities.description_detailed: Removes an emoji reaction that was previously added to a message.

channel_id.description: Channel ID where the message is

capabilities.name: user.list
capabilities.description: List all users in the workspace
capabilities.description_detailed: Lists all active users in the Slack workspace. Excludes deleted/deactivated users.

limit.description: Maximum number of users to return

users.description: List of user objects with id, name, email, is_bot, is_admin

capabilities.name: user.info
capabilities.description: Get detailed information about a user
capabilities.description_detailed: Retrieves detailed profile information for a specific Slack user including name, email, title, phone, and timezone.

capabilities.name: search.messages
capabilities.description: Search for messages in the workspace
capabilities.description_detailed: Searches for messages across the workspace using Slack's search syntax. Supports operators like 'from:', 'in:', 'has:', etc.

messages.description: List of matching messages with text, user, channel, permalink
